text: But Sergio, you want to talk more about your tech stack?
rationale: Question/handoff to another panelist, not advice

text: So if after this you want to talk with me about how IEEE is saving the world, I would love to tell you in more detail.
rationale: Invitation to chat later, not advice

text: And if you want to hear my interviews, I guess you just have to give me a follow.
rationale: Self-promo CTA (follow me), not advice

text: And so today you know today I think we were even seeing some of the space stocks come down as SpaceX lowered their I don't know their targets their guidance whatever you want to call that late last week.
rationale: Market commentary; 'you want to' matched mid-sentence, not advice

text: Would you want to see a demo of that product?
rationale: Question ('would you want to see a demo?'), not advice

text: And if you want to join us, we will have an happy hour.
rationale: Logistics invite (happy hour), not advice

text: It sort of became trivial to like, you know, develop one of these things, like a very cheap, and it's amazing to now, just a couple years later, you know, the whole S-curve is reset, and it turns out that if you have a PhD in, you know, machine learning, you're the shit, you know, and in fact, you can name your product whatever you want, including random letters, and people will adopt it, and you will make billions of dollars per year on you.
rationale: Vague enthusiasm; 'it turns out' trailing, not a market reframe

text: So just real fast, in case I'm sure a lot of people here know what MCP is, we basically can kind of think of it as like a Swiss Army knife, right?
rationale: Teaching analogy (Swiss Army knife), not a market reframe

text: I taught myself how to code, and I taught myself how to build it so that I could build it, and then it turns out it's pretty cool, and it works pretty well, so other people can use it now.
rationale: Personal anecdote ('it turns out it's cool'), not a reframe

text: Think of it as a testbed.
rationale: Analogy ('think of it as a testbed'), not a reframe

text: And it turns out, you know, our technology was perfectly aligned with what the government was looking for.
rationale: Self-narrative about tech alignment, not a reframe

text: That said, you know, we always say the magic of Tech Week is being in person.
rationale: Discourse transition + promo ('that said... magic of Tech Week'), not a caveat

text: And what undid me was there was, and this is, I'm not blaming id, there was a setting that said if you're player hosted, this must be a LAN game.
rationale: 'that said' matched inside a story, not a caveat

text: I think it depends on states.
rationale: Bare 'it depends' filler, no substantive caveat

text: Yeah, it depends on the fund model, to your point.
rationale: 'it depends on the fund model' hedge filler, not a caveat

text: So it depends.
rationale: 'So it depends.' pure filler, not a caveat

text: Can you hear me?
rationale: Logistical check ('can you hear me?'), not a substantive question

text: Let's say good, right?
rationale: Rhetorical tag ('let's say good, right?'), not an open question

text: Like, this is the noise, right?
rationale: Rhetorical tag ('this is the noise, right?'), not an open question

text: Who feels really strongly about New York?
rationale: Audience-engagement prompt, not a substantive open question

text: Okay, we're going to take a quick break and be back in about ten minutes.
rationale: Logistics announcement injected as an advice candidate

text: Thanks everyone for coming out tonight, the bar is open in the back.
rationale: Housekeeping/thanks injected as a reframe candidate

text: My name is Dana and I run partnerships at an early-stage fund.
rationale: Self-introduction injected as a caveat candidate

text: It was really nice catching up with you about the weather earlier.
rationale: Small talk (declarative) injected as a question candidate

text: The wifi password is on the screen behind me if you need it.
rationale: Logistics injected as an advice candidate
